name: Smoking Salmon and Trout Part i - Caring for the Catch
recipeInstructions:
1. Immediately remove the slime and scales from the fish if possible.
2. Cut open the throat and carefully remove the gills.
3. Remove the intestines; save the liver, roe, milt, kidney, and covering membrane for later use.
4. If space allows, detach the head. Leave the lug bone attached if you plan to smoke the fish as it will support the handling cord.
5. Cool the fish quickly using chipped ice, ensuring there is a layer of ice between each layer of fish in the cooler.
6. Handle the fish gently with a net or by gaffing the least valuable part (e.g., stomach) to avoid bruising.
7. If using a fish club for quick dispatch, deliver one sharp blow to the head.
8. Be aware of rigor mortis: after death, the fish will stiffen but should relax over time unless it enters accelerated rigor from being too warm. Avoid disturbing the flesh during this period to prevent juice loss and appearance issues.
9. Use dry ice or eutectic ice (23% salt by weight and 77% water by weight) for transporting frozen fish, as regular melting ice will thaw the fish.
Note: This recipe is extracted from "Smoking Salmon & Trout" by Jack Whelan, published by Airie Publishing, Deep Bay, B.C. ISBN: 0-919807-00-3.
